{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01CKNUbmdsL8WLwLbrq5Y5os","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"## Fault Localization Summary\n\nI've successfully identified the source of the iwlwifi firmware loading bug:\n\n### **Buggy File**\n- **Path**: `drivers/net/wireless/intel/iwlwifi/pcie/drv.c`\n\n### **Buggy Data Structure**\n- **Name**: `iwl_dev_info_table` (lines 978-997)\n- **Type**: Static const array mapping PCI device/subdevice IDs to device configurations\n\n### **Root Cause**\nThe `iwl_dev_info_table` is missing three PCI subdevice entries (0x4010, 0x4018, 0x401C) for device ID 0x2526. This causes the driver probe function (`iwl_pci_probe`) to fail to find a matching device configuration at runtime (lines 1031-1042). Without a specific configuration match, the device falls back to a generic configuration that doesn't load the proper firmware.\n\n### **Key Evidence**\n1. **Existing pattern**: Device 0x2526 with subdevice 0x4030 uses `iwl9560_2ac_160_cfg` configuration (line 995)\n2. **First table**: Device 0x2526 has entries for 0x4034 but NOT for 0x4010, 0x4018, 0x401C\n3. **Device matching logic**: The matching loop iterates through `iwl_dev_info_table` to find device-specific configs (lines 1031-1042)\n4. **Missing entries affect**: Subdevice IDs 0x4010, 0x4018, and 0x401C of device 0x2526\n\nThe result has been written to `/workspace/fault_localization_result.json` with high confidence (0.95)."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":9,"cache_creation_input_tokens":722,"cache_read_input_tokens":44668,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":722},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6e6251ae-ed2c-42a7-b11c-b16a100c4d65","uuid":"a381fe6e-3645-495b-9d2a-bd2e0508bfc8"}
